findings.title: Chunked background sends can still exceed Discord's 2000-character limit
findings.description: The new worker-side chunking assumes `formatter::split_message(..., 2000)` always returns Discord-safe chunks, but it does not. In `split_message`, the force-split path appends closing code fences after slicing at `max_len`, so a chunk can end up longer than 2000 characters. That means long background tool output wrapped in fenced Markdown can still be rejected by `channel_id.say`, so this diff does not fully fix the delivery failure it is targeting.
findings.suggestion: Route these sends through a single helper that enforces the final Discord payload limit after formatting, or fix `split_message` so every returned chunk is guaranteed to be `<= max_len` even when reopening/closing code fences.

findings.title: Background chunking now multiplies push notifications
findings.description: These new loops send each chunk with `channel_id.say`, which does not set `SUPPRESS_NOTIFICATIONS`. Before this change, an oversized background notification failed once; after this change, the same logical event can arrive as several separate notifying messages. For long background tool output, that turns one event into multiple user-visible notifications and is likely to feel like notification spam.
findings.suggestion: Use the same silent-send path as `say_silent` in `message.rs`, or build `CreateMessage` with `MessageFlags::SUPPRESS_NOTIFICATIONS` for worker-side chunked sends as well.

findings.title: Repeated inline send loops duplicate delivery policy and drift from the main message path
findings.description: The diff copies near-identical chunk-and-send loops into several worker branches instead of reusing one helper. That increases the chance of inconsistent behavior across paths: some sends are silent, some are not; some have a shared limit constant, some hardcode `2000`; some will benefit from future fixes, some will not. This is already visible between `say_silent` in `message.rs` and the worker code.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared Discord send helper for both foreground and background paths that applies the same size limit, flags, logging, and any future retry/rate-limit policy.

summary: The diff improves oversized-message handling, but worker-side delivery still has a real edge-case failure for fenced long content and now risks notification spam because chunked background sends are not silent.
